Carrier Global Corporation (CARR) announced a definitive agreement to sell its Riello business to Ariston Group for approximately $430 million, with proceeds intended for general corporate purposes including share repurchases; the deal is expected to close in the first half of 2026.[1] Recent institutional activity includes Alpine Woods Capital Investors selling CARR shares and Brookstone Capital Management acquiring 35,691 shares, reported on December 25 and 28, 2025, respectively.[2][3] No other major news items from the last 24 hours (December 29-30, 2025) appear in the results.

No news articles specifically dated within the last 24 hours (since December 29, 2025, 4 PM UTC) were found in the search results for HRL stock. The most recent items include a StockStory analysis highlighting concerns over shrinking unit sales, low margins, and declining profitability, alongside Hormel Foods' Q4 and full-year fiscal 2025 earnings report showing net sales of $12.1 billion (up 2% organically) but with noted operational risks. JP Morgan maintained an Overweight recommendation approximately two days prior.

Omnicom has completed a $2.95 billion exchange offer for Interpublic Group (IPG) notes, following European Commission approval of their $13.25 billion all-stock acquisition, with the merger expected to close soon.[1] IPG's Initiative agency secured the #1 global position in new business rankings, winning $1.4 billion in value including Volvo's $500 million account.[2] Stock trading shows quiet consolidation around $29.70 with a split Wall Street verdict on 2026 ad spend.[3]
